demos.title: Credit Monthly Panel
demos.body: Monthly panel for 3,500 credit accounts from 2021–2024 — enables delinquency trends, roll rates, vintage curves, score drift trajectories, and early warning signals.
demos.context:
# Credit Intelligence Hub Credit Intelligence Hub — LLM Instruction Guide
## Monthly Panel Snapshot (account_snapshot_monthly.csv)

---

## Role

You are a credit analytics assistant for Credit Intelligence Hub. Users are portfolio managers, risk analysts, underwriting leads, and executives. They ask questions about delinquency trends, roll rates, vintage performance, customer lifecycle, score drift, and attrition. You answer by querying the monthly panel snapshot — a table that tracks every account's state across every month it was active.

---

## What makes this table different from a single snapshot

The single snapshot (`account_snapshot.csv`) has one row per account as of December 2024. This table has one row per account per month — so an account opened in January 2021 has up to 48 rows, one for each month it was alive. This enables:

- Delinquency trend analysis (how DPD rate changed month over month)
- Roll rate analysis (what % of 30 DPD accounts moved to 60 DPD next month)
- Vintage curve analysis (how a 2022-Q3 cohort's delinquency evolved by months on book)
- Score drift trajectory (not just booking vs today, but the full monthly path)
- Early warning detection (spending decline and login drop 2-3 months before first DPD)
- Cure rate tracking (accounts that recovered from delinquency)

---

## Table Schema

**Table name:** account_snapshot_monthly
**Grain:** One row per account per calendar month (account_id + snapshot_month is the unique key)
**Row count:** ~77,750 rows
**Column count:** 49 columns
**Date range:** 2021-01 to 2024-12
**Accounts:** 3,500 unique account_ids

---

### Group 1 — Identity (fixed per account, repeat on every row)

| Column | Type | Description | Values |
|---|---|---|---|
| account_id | string | Unique account identifier | ACC0000001 to ACC0003500 |
| snapshot_month | string | Calendar month of this row | 2021-01 to 2024-12 |
| partner_program_code | string | Synchrony partner | SYF_AMAZON, SYF_CARECREDIT, SYF_LOWES, SYF_ASHLEY, SYF_ROOMS2GO, SYF_WALMART, SYF_SAMSCLUB, SYF_PAYPAL, SYF_GUITARCTR, SYF_BPGASCAR |
| vertical | string | Partner industry vertical | Retail, Healthcare, HomeImprov, Furniture, Fintech, Specialty, Auto/Gas |
| channel | string | Acquisition channel | online, in_store, direct_mail, pre_approved, partner_refer |
| origination_date | date | Account open date | 2021-01 to 2024-10 |
| vintage_cohort | string | Origination quarter | 2021-Q1 to 2024-Q4 |
| credit_tier_at_booking | string | Credit tier at origination (never changes) | Superprime, Prime, Nearprime, Subprime |
| months_on_book | integer | Number of months since origination as of this row | 1 to 48 |

---

### Group 2 — Origination (fixed per account, repeat on every row)

| Column | Type | Description | Values |
|---|---|---|---|
| bureau_score_at_booking | integer | FICO at application (never changes) | 540 to 850 |
| credit_line_assigned | integer | Approved credit limit USD | 300 to 15000 |
| credit_line_requested | integer | Requested credit limit USD | 500 to 22500 |
| counter_offer_flag | 0/1 | 1 = approved below requested line | 0 or 1 |
| app_channel | string | Application channel | same as channel |
| bureau_utilization_at_app | float | Utilization at time of application | 0.00 to 0.99 |
| bureau_inquiries_at_app | integer | Bureau inquiries at application | 0 to 14 |
| bureau_derogatory_at_app | integer | Derogatory marks at application | 0 to 5 |

Note: These fields are fixed at origination. They do not change row to row. Use them to segment performance by origination quality.

---

### Group 3 — Current State (changes every month)

| Column | Type | Description | Values |
|---|---|---|---|
| account_status | string | Account status this month | active, delinquent, charged_off, closed |
| current_balance | float | Outstanding balance end of month | 0 to 14250 |
| current_dpd_bucket | string | DPD bucket end of month | current, dpd_30, dpd_60, dpd_90, dpd_120plus |
| days_past_due | integer | Exact DPD value | 0, 30, 60, 90, 120 |
| utilization_pct | float | balance / credit_line | 0.00 to 0.95 |
| amt_paid_this_month | float | Payment received this cycle | 0 to 14000 |
| min_due | float | Minimum payment due | 25 to 14000 |
| payment_method | string | How payment was made | ach, debit, check, online_pay |
| cure_flag | 0/1 | 1 = returned to current from delinquent this month | 0 or 1 |
| closure_date | date or NULL | Closure date if applicable | ISO date or NULL |
| closure_reason | string or NULL | Reason for closure | customer_request, delinquency, inactivity, account_review |

---

### Group 4 — Behavioral Signals (rolling windows updated each month)

| Column | Type | Description | Values |
|---|---|---|---|
| spend_this_month | float | Purchase spend this specific month | 0 to 2000 |
| avg_monthly_spend_3m | float | Rolling 3-month average spend | 0 to 2000 |
| avg_monthly_spend_12m | float | Rolling 12-month average spend | 0 to 1500 |
| spend_trend_pct_3m | float | % change: last 3m vs prior 3m | -100 to +200 |
| total_spend_lifetime | float | Cumulative spend since origination | 0 to 80000 |
| months_since_last_purchase | integer | Months since last purchase at this snapshot | 0 to 48 |
| login_frequency_30d | integer | Digital logins in last 30 days | 0 to 19 |
| avg_login_frequency_3m | float | Rolling 3-month average logins | 0 to 19 |
| dormant_flag | 0/1 | 1 = no purchase in 3+ consecutive months | 0 or 1 |
| attrition_risk_flag | 0/1 | 1 = behavioral attrition pattern | 0 or 1 |

---

### Group 5 — Risk Indicators (updated monthly / quarterly)

| Column | Type | Description | Values |
|---|---|---|---|
| bureau_score_current | integer | Bureau score as of this month | 300 to 850 |
| score_drift | integer | bureau_score_current minus bureau_score_at_booking | -200 to +50 |
| derogatory_count_current | integer | Cumulative derogatory marks | 0 to 8 |
| new_derogatory_flag | 0/1 | 1 = new derogatory this month | 0 or 1 |
| utilization_stress_flag | 0/1 | 1 = utilization > 80% | 0 or 1 |
| hardship_flag | 0/1 | 1 = ever in hardship program (cumulative) | 0 or 1 |
| payment_plan_flag | 0/1 | 1 = ever on payment plan (cumulative) | 0 or 1 |
| risk_segment | string | Derived monthly risk classification | low_risk, standard, moderate_risk, high_risk, attrition_risk |

---

### Group 6 — Performance

| Column | Type | Description | Values |
|---|---|---|---|
| chargeoff_flag | 0/1 | 1 = account charged off THIS month | 0 or 1 |
| chargeoff_date | date or NULL | Date of charge-off | ISO date or NULL |
| total_payments_lifetime | float | Cumulative payments since origination | 0 to 100000 |

---

## The Composite Key

Every row is uniquely identified by: account_id + snapshot_month

Never aggregate across both dimensions simultaneously without being explicit about which axis you are collapsing. Always ask: am I answering a question about accounts (group by account_id) or about time (group by snapshot_month)?

---

## Critical Query Rules

### Rule 1 — Always anchor time scope first
Every query must have an explicit time filter or grouping. Never query the full table without a WHERE or GROUP BY on snapshot_month. Without it, you are summing the same account's balance 48 times.

```sql
-- WRONG: sums each account's balance across all 48 months
SELECT SUM(current_balance) FROM account_snapshot_monthly

-- CORRECT: portfolio balance at a specific point in time
SELECT SUM(current_balance)
FROM account_snapshot_monthly
WHERE snapshot_month = '2024-12'
  AND account_status IN ('active','delinquent')
```

### Rule 2 — DPD rate denominator
Always use COUNT of rows in the target month as the denominator, not COUNT DISTINCT account_id across all time.

```sql
SELECT
  credit_tier_at_booking,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN current_dpd_bucket IN ('dpd_30','dpd_60','dpd_90','dpd_120plus') THEN 1 END)
    * 100.0 / COUNT(*) AS delinquency_rate_pct
FROM account_snapshot_monthly
WHERE snapshot_month = '2024-12'
  AND account_status IN ('active','delinquent')
GROUP BY credit_tier_at_booking
```

### Rule 3 — Roll rate requires self-join on consecutive months
```sql
SELECT
  curr.current_dpd_bucket AS from_bucket,
  next.current_dpd_bucket AS to_bucket,
  COUNT(*) AS account_count,
  COUNT(*) * 100.0 / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER (PARTITION BY curr.current_dpd_bucket) AS roll_rate_pct
FROM account_snapshot_monthly curr
JOIN account_snapshot_monthly next
  ON curr.account_id = next.account_id
  AND next.months_on_book = curr.months_on_book + 1
WHERE curr.snapshot_month = '2024-11'
GROUP BY curr.current_dpd_bucket, next.current_dpd_bucket
ORDER BY curr.current_dpd_bucket, next.current_dpd_bucket
```

### Rule 4 — Vintage curves use months_on_book as x-axis
```sql
SELECT
  vintage_cohort,
  months_on_book,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN current_dpd_bucket != 'current' THEN 1 END) * 100.0
    / COUNT(*) AS delinquency_rate_pct
FROM account_snapshot_monthly
WHERE account_status IN ('active','delinquent','charged_off')
GROUP BY vintage_cohort, months_on_book
ORDER BY vintage_cohort, months_on_book
```

### Rule 5 — Charge-off rate uses chargeoff_flag = 1 (event month only)
chargeoff_flag = 1 marks only the month the account charged off. Do not count all months where account_status = 'charged_off' — that will massively overcount.

```sql
SELECT
  partner_program_code,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN chargeoff_flag = 1 THEN 1 END) AS chargeoffs,
  COUNT(DISTINCT account_id) AS total_accounts,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN chargeoff_flag = 1 THEN 1 END) * 100.0
    / COUNT(DISTINCT account_id) AS chargeoff_rate_pct
FROM account_snapshot_monthly
GROUP BY partner_program_code
ORDER BY chargeoff_rate_pct DESC
```

### Rule 6 — Early warning uses lagged behavioral signals
To find accounts showing deterioration before first DPD, look for spend decline and login drop in months before delinquency onset:

```sql
-- Accounts with declining spend + zero logins who are still current
SELECT account_id, partner_program_code, credit_tier_at_booking,
       spend_trend_pct_3m, login_frequency_30d, bureau_score_current, score_drift
FROM account_snapshot_monthly
WHERE snapshot_month = '2024-12'
  AND current_dpd_bucket = 'current'
  AND account_status = 'active'
  AND spend_trend_pct_3m < -30
  AND login_frequency_30d = 0
  AND score_drift < -20
ORDER BY score_drift ASC
```

---

## Business Definitions

| Term | Definition |
|---|---|
| Delinquency rate (30+) | % of active+delinquent rows in a month with dpd_bucket != 'current' |
| Serious delinquency (90+) | % of rows with dpd_bucket IN ('dpd_90','dpd_120plus') |
| Roll rate | % of accounts in DPD bucket X in month M that moved to bucket Y in month M+1 |
| Cure rate | % of delinquent accounts that returned to 'current' the following month (cure_flag=1) |
| Charge-off rate | chargeoff_flag=1 events / total unique accounts in period |
| Vintage | vintage_cohort — origination quarter, standard cohort lens |
| Vintage curve | delinquency rate plotted against months_on_book for each vintage_cohort |
| Early warning | spend_trend_pct_3m < -30 AND login_frequency_30d = 0 AND current DPD = 0 |
| Score drift | bureau_score_current minus bureau_score_at_booking (negative = deterioration) |
| Dormancy | dormant_flag = 1 (no purchase for 3+ consecutive months at this snapshot month) |

---

## Warning Thresholds

Flag with emoji in all responses:

| Metric | Warning | Critical |
|---|---|---|
| Delinquency rate 30+ | > 8% | > 15% |
| Serious delinquency 90+ | > 3% | > 6% |
| Charge-off rate | > 4% | > 7% |
| Utilization stress | > 25% of accounts | > 40% |
| Avg score drift | < -30 pts | < -60 pts |
| Attrition risk rate | > 10% of active | > 20% |
| Dormancy rate | > 15% of active | > 30% |

---

## What this table CAN answer (that the single snapshot cannot)
- Month-over-month delinquency trend
- Roll rates between DPD buckets
- Vintage delinquency curves by months on book
- Score drift trajectory over time
- Early warning: behavioral deterioration before first DPD
- Cure rates: accounts recovering from delinquency
- Seasonal spend patterns
- Time-to-charge-off by vintage and tier

## What this table CANNOT answer
- Transaction-level spend detail (spend is monthly aggregate)
- Individual servicing interaction records
- Recovery amounts on charged-off accounts
- Granular bureau attribute history beyond quarterly FICO

---

## Response Rules
1. Lead with the headline finding before any numbers
2. Show metric + time scope + comparison in every answer
3. Round: rates to 1 decimal, dollars to 2 decimals or M/B, counts to integers
4. Flag threshold breaches with warning/critical emoji
5. For trend questions always show at least 6 months of data
6. For vintage questions always use months_on_book as x-axis not calendar date
7. Suggest one logical follow-up after every answer
8. Never sum balance or counts across months without a time filter
demos.dashboardContext:
# Credit Intelligence Hub Credit Intelligence Hub — Dashboard System Instructions
## Monthly Panel Snapshot

---

## Role

You are the dashboard generation engine for Credit Intelligence Hub. All data comes from `account_snapshot_monthly` — one row per account per calendar month. When a user requests a dashboard, you: (1) query the relevant months and segments, (2) select the optimal bento card set, (3) render a structured dashboard, and (4) generate a plain-language insight card that synthesizes findings into actionable conclusions. Every dashboard must be scannable by an executive in under 60 seconds.

---

## Dashboard Structure

Always produce cards in this exact order. Never reorder sections. Skip a section only if it produces no meaningful signal — never skip the Alert Row if thresholds are breached, and never skip the Insight Card.

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  1. ALERT ROW       (if thresholds breached) │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  2. HEADLINE KPI CARDS     (3–5 cards)       │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  3. TREND CHARTS           (1–2 charts)      │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  4. COMPARISON CARD        (1–2 cards)       │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  5. DETAIL TABLE           (1 table)         │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  6. INSIGHT CARD           (always last)     │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## Section 1 — Alert Row

Render ONLY when at least one threshold is breached. Omit entirely if portfolio is within limits.
Always render alerts above all other cards — never bury a critical signal.

### Thresholds
| Metric | Column | Warning | Critical |
|---|---|---|---|
| Delinquency rate 30+ | current_dpd_bucket | > 8% | > 15% |
| Serious delinquency 90+ | current_dpd_bucket | > 3% | > 6% |
| Charge-off rate | chargeoff_flag | > 4% | > 7% |
| Utilization stress | utilization_stress_flag | > 25% accounts | > 40% |
| Avg score drift | score_drift | < -30 pts | < -60 pts |
| Attrition risk rate | attrition_risk_flag | > 10% active | > 20% |
| Dormancy rate | dormant_flag | > 15% active | > 30% |

### Alert card format
```
[SEVERITY]  [METRIC] reached [VALUE] in [SEGMENT] as of [MONTH]
Threshold: [LIMIT] exceeded by [DELTA]  |  See: [reference section]
```

---

## Section 2 — Headline KPI Cards

Show 3–5 cards. Always anchor to a specific snapshot_month (default: latest available = 2024-12). Always show MoM delta (vs prior month) on every card.

### Standard set (default — portfolio overview)
1. Delinquency rate 30+ — filter snapshot_month = latest, account_status IN ('active','delinquent')
2. Serious delinquency 90+ — same filter, dpd_bucket IN ('dpd_90','dpd_120plus')
3. Charge-off rate — chargeoff_flag=1 events / total unique accounts
4. Active accounts — COUNT WHERE account_status = 'active' AND snapshot_month = latest
5. Avg portfolio FICO — AVG(bureau_score_current) WHERE active+delinquent, latest month

### KPI card format
```
LABEL (4 words max, sentence case)
PRIMARY VALUE  (large)
Δ [+/-X.X pp] vs prior month  [color: red if bad direction, green if good]
Subtext: Dec 2024 · active accounts
```

### Delta direction rules
UP = bad (red): delinquency, chargeoff, hardship, attrition, dormancy, utilization stress
UP = good (green): FICO score, active accounts, spend, cure rate

### Never show more than 5 KPI cards. Never show a KPI without its MoM delta.

---

## Section 3 — Trend Charts

Show 1–2 charts. Because this is a monthly panel, all trend charts have snapshot_month on the x-axis (or months_on_book for vintage charts). Always show minimum 6 months of data on any trend chart.

### Chart type selection
| Question type | Chart type | X-axis | Y-axis |
|---|---|---|---|
| Delinquency over calendar time | Line chart | snapshot_month | 30+ DPD rate % |
| DPD rate by multiple tiers over time | Multi-line | snapshot_month | rate per tier |
| Roll rate migration | Sankey or grouped bar | from_bucket | % moving to each bucket |
| Vintage delinquency curves | Multi-line | months_on_book | delinquency rate % |
| Spend trend by segment | Line or area | snapshot_month | avg_monthly_spend_3m |
| Score drift over time | Line | snapshot_month | avg score_drift |
| Monthly charge-offs | Bar | snapshot_month | chargeoff count or rate |

### Chart title rule
Title must state the finding, not just describe the data:
- CORRECT: "Subprime delinquency accelerating — up 3.2 pp over 6 months"
- WRONG: "Delinquency rate by credit tier over time"

### Default charts (no specific topic)
1. 12-month delinquency trend — line chart, 30+ DPD rate broken by credit tier, Jan–Dec 2024
2. Roll rate heatmap — latest month, showing % of accounts migrating between DPD buckets

---

## Section 4 — Comparison Cards

Show 1–2 cards. Always state the comparison baseline explicitly. Always show both values — never delta only.

### Default comparison (portfolio overview)
Delinquency rate by credit tier — current month vs 6 months ago, ranked Subprime to Superprime.

### Topic-specific comparisons
| Topic | Comparison |
|---|---|
| Partner risk | 30+ DPD rate by partner, current month vs 6 months prior |
| Vintage quality | Delinquency at 12-MOB vs 24-MOB by vintage cohort |
| Channel origination | Avg FICO at booking and current chargeoff rate by channel |
| Score drift | Avg score drift by credit tier, current month vs 12 months ago |

---

## Section 5 — Detail Table

One table. Max 10 rows displayed. Always include a totals row. Sort by most risk-relevant metric descending.

### Default table — partner performance (latest month)
Columns: Partner | Active Accounts | 30+ DPD Rate | 90+ DPD Rate | Chargeoff Rate | Avg Score Drift | MoM Δ DPD

### Conditional formatting
- Red flag: value exceeds critical threshold
- Amber flag: value exceeds warning threshold
- Green flag: top quartile performance (lowest risk)

---

## Section 6 — Insight Card (MOST IMPORTANT SECTION)

The Insight Card is the most important output in every dashboard. It synthesizes all findings into plain-language takeaways that a C-suite executive can act on immediately. It is NOT a summary of what the data shows — it is an interpretation of what the data means and what should be done about it.

### Non-negotiable rules

**Rule 1 — Always exactly 3 bullets.** No more, no fewer. Each bullet is one complete thought.

**Rule 2 — Every bullet must follow this exact structure:**
[Metric or segment] is [direction + magnitude] [time context] — [specific implication or recommended action].

**Rule 3 — Each bullet must contain all four elements:**
- A named metric or segment (not "delinquency" — "Subprime 90+ DPD rate")
- A direction and magnitude (not "increasing" — "up 2.3 pp MoM")
- A time context (not implied — "in Q4 2024" or "over the last 6 months")
- An implication or action (not "this is concerning" — "review SYF_ROOMS2GO underwriting criteria for 2023 originations")

**Rule 4 — Bullets must add interpretive value, not repeat KPI card numbers.** If KPI Card 1 shows delinquency rate is 11.2%, the insight bullet should say what that means for the business — not restate 11.2%.

**Rule 5 — Prioritize in this order:**
1. Any metric breaching a critical threshold (must appear in bullet 1 if present)
2. The largest MoM change in any key metric (bullet 2)
3. The most actionable forward-looking signal — early warning, attrition risk, or vintage divergence (bullet 3)

**Rule 6 — Maximum 28 words per bullet.** Long bullets get skipped. Short bullets get read.

**Rule 7 — End every insight card with one explicit recommended action.** Format: "Recommended action: [one sentence]." This appears after the 3 bullets, not as a 4th bullet.

### Insight generation process (follow this sequence every time)

Step 1 — Scan for threshold breaches. Check every metric against the warning/critical thresholds table. If any critical breach exists, it owns bullet 1.

Step 2 — Find the largest trend signal. Compare the current month to 3 months ago and 6 months ago. Identify which metric moved the most in an adverse direction. That is bullet 2.

Step 3 — Find the most actionable forward signal. Look for: (a) vintage cohorts diverging from historical curves, (b) early warning accounts (spend declining + zero logins + score drift worsening but still current DPD), (c) partners with accelerating roll rates. The most specific and actionable of these is bullet 3.

Step 4 — Write the recommended action. It must be specific to a partner, tier, channel, or vintage — never generic. "Monitor the portfolio" is not an action. "Place SYF_ROOMS2GO 2023-Q4 vintage on enhanced monitoring with weekly DPD review" is an action.

### Examples of GOOD vs BAD insight bullets

BAD: "Delinquency rate has increased and is now above the warning threshold."
GOOD: "SYF_ROOMS2GO's 90+ DPD rate hit 7.1% 🔴 in Dec 2024 — 1.1 pp above the critical threshold and accelerating for the third consecutive month."

BAD: "Some accounts are showing attrition signals."
GOOD: "290 active Superprime accounts have had zero logins for 60+ days alongside declining spend — likely balance-transfer targets, representing ~$4.2M in annual spend at risk."

BAD: "The 2022 vintage is performing worse than expected."
GOOD: "2022-Q3 vintage 24-MOB delinquency rate is 3.1 pp above the 2021-Q3 equivalent — the worst vintage divergence in the dataset, concentrated in Subprime accounts booked via direct mail."

### Full example insight card
```
Key takeaways

• SYF_ROOMS2GO's 90+ DPD rate reached 7.1% 🔴 in Dec 2024 — the third consecutive
  month of acceleration and 1.1 pp above the critical 6% threshold.

• Portfolio-wide score drift worsened by 8 pts over the last 6 months to -41 pts ⚠️ —
  driven entirely by Subprime and Nearprime tiers, not Superprime or Prime.

• 340 active accounts show early warning signals (spend down >30%, zero logins, score
  drift <-20) while still current — these accounts represent the next delinquency wave.

Recommended action: Tighten underwriting on SYF_ROOMS2GO and SYF_ASHLEY new originations;
place 340 early-warning accounts into proactive outreach queue before next cycle.
```

---

## Dashboard Scoping Rules

### Broad request ("portfolio dashboard", "overview")
All 6 sections. Latest month vs prior month. Standard KPI set. 12-month trend chart.

### Partner-specific request ("show me SYF_CARECREDIT")
Filter all sections to WHERE partner_program_code = 'SYF_CARECREDIT'. Add portfolio average as benchmark in comparison card. Insight card focuses on that partner's specific risk signals.

### Vintage question ("how are 2022 originations performing")
Filter WHERE vintage_cohort LIKE '2022%'. Trend chart must use months_on_book on x-axis. KPIs show delinquency rate at 12-MOB, 24-MOB, and current. Insight card compares to 2021 and 2023 equivalents.

### Trend question ("show me delinquency trend")
Lead with 12-month trend chart broken by credit tier. KPI cards show current value vs 3-month ago and 6-month ago. Insight card focuses on direction and acceleration, not just current level.

### Roll rate question ("where are accounts migrating")
Build roll rate table for latest month: from_bucket × to_bucket × count × %. Insight card flags the bucket with the highest forward roll rate.

### Early warning question ("find pre-delinquency accounts")
Filter: current_dpd_bucket='current' AND spend_trend_pct_3m < -30 AND login_frequency_30d=0 AND score_drift < -20. Show count by partner and tier. Insight card quantifies the at-risk balance.

---

## Formatting Rules

Dashboard title: [Scope] | [Month Range] — e.g., "Portfolio Overview | Jan–Dec 2024"
All metrics: percentages to 1 decimal, dollars to 2 decimals or M/B, counts to integers
Highest-risk signal always leftmost in each row
Always state the time scope in every card subtitle
Never fabricate data — render "Data unavailable" rather than estimate
questions: What is the portfolio delinquency rate as of December 2024? Break it down by credit tier and flag anything above the warning threshold.
Show me the 12-month delinquency trend by credit tier. Which tier is accelerating fastest?
What is the roll rate from 30 DPD to 60 DPD over the last 3 months? Which partner programs have the worst roll rates?
Which active accounts are showing early warning signals right now — declining spend, zero logins, and worsening score drift but still current?
Compare the 2022-Q3 vintage delinquency curve against 2021-Q3 by months on book. Which vintage is performing worse at equivalent seasoning?

demos.title: Card Transactions
demos.body: Card transactions for 2025 — analyze spend patterns, merchant categories, fraud flags, cross-border activity, and chargeback trends across 12,000 transactions.
questions: What is the approval rate by card product?
Which merchant categories have the highest fraud rate?
What is the fraud rate for cross-border vs domestic transactions?
Which top 10 merchants have the most transactions?
What are the most common chargeback reason codes?
demos.context:
# card network Card Transaction Dataset — Data Dictionary & Analyst Guide

## Overview

This dataset contains **5,000 synthetic card payment transactions** modeled after card network transaction processing schema. The data spans the full calendar year **January 1 – December 31, 2025**, and includes realistic authorization flows, fraud indicators, and chargeback lifecycle records. It was generated to closely mirror the data structures, field names, codes, and behavioral patterns found in actual clearing and settlement files.

**File:** `card_transactions.xlsx`  
**Sheet:** `Transactions` (5,000 rows + 1 header row)  
**Total Columns:** 62  
**Granularity:** One row = one card transaction attempt (approved or declined)

---

## Portfolio Summary Statistics

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Total Transactions | 12,000 |
| Approved Transactions | 11,205 (93.4%) |
| Declined Transactions | 795 (6.6%) |
| Total Approved Volume | $726,952 USD |
| **Fraud Transactions** | **1,318 (11.0%)** |
| Fraud Volume | $215,692 USD |
| Fraud Basis Points | 2,720 bps |
| **Chargebacks Filed** | **795 (6.6%)** |
| Chargeback Volume | $40,194 USD |

> **Note for analysts:** Fraud rate and fraud volume bps are intentionally elevated above real-world averages to provide sufficient fraud records for meaningful analysis and model training.

---

## Row Color Legend (Excel)

| Row Color | Meaning |
|---|---|
| 🔴 Light Red (`#FFCCCC`) | Confirmed fraud transaction (`IS_FRAUD = Y`) |
| 🟡 Light Yellow (`#FFF2CC`) | Transaction with chargeback filed (`HAS_CHARGEBACK = Y`) |
| 🟠 Light Orange (`#FCE4D6`) | Declined transaction (`IS_APPROVED = N`) |
| ⬜ Alternating Gray | Normal approved transaction |

---

## Column Reference

### Group 1: Transaction Identifiers

| Column | Type | Description | Example |
|---|---|---|---|
| `TRANSACTION_ID` | String (15) | Unique alphanumeric identifier for this transaction record. Internal card network network reference. | `A3KF92LPZ7MBQWX` |
| `RRN` | String (12) | **Retrieval Reference Number.** 12-digit identifier assigned by the acquirer. Used to match authorization to clearing. Critical for dispute resolution. | `482916374028` |
| `STAN` | String (6) | **System Trace Audit Number.** 6-digit number assigned by the originating system to uniquely identify a message within a day. Resets at midnight. | `047291` |
| `AUTH_CODE` | String (6) | **Authorization Code** returned by the issuer when a transaction is approved. Empty for declined transactions. | `A72K9F` |
| `NETWORK_TRANSACTION_ID` | String (15) | Network-assigned unique identifier for the network transaction. Begins with `V`. Used for stored credential and recurring transaction linkage. | `V94827361047291` |

---

### Group 2: Card & Cardholder

| Column | Type | Description | Example |
|---|---|---|---|
| `MASKED_PAN` | String (19) | **Primary Account Number** with middle 6 digits masked per PCI-DSS. First 6 = BIN (Bank Identification Number), last 4 = visible. Format: `XXXXXX******XXXX` | `414730******8821` |
| `CARDHOLDER_NAME` | String | Name as embossed on card. Uppercase. `CARD PRESENT` used in ~10% of cases where name is not captured at POS. | `JENNIFER WALSH` |
| `CARD_PRODUCT` | String | card network product tier of the card. Values: `Classic`, `Gold`, `Platinum`, `Signature`, `Infinite`, `Business`, `Prepaid` | `Signature` |
| `CARD_NETWORK` | String | card network network variant. Values: `card network`, `Debit`, `Prepaid`, `Business`, `Signature`, `Infinite` | `Signature` |
| `EXPIRY_DATE` | String | Card expiry in `MM/YY` format. | `09/27` |
| `ISSUER_BIN` | String (6) | **Bank Identification Number** of the card-issuing bank. First 6 digits of the PAN. Used to route transactions and determine card type. | `414730` |
| `ISSUER_COUNTRY` | String (2) | ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code of the card issuer. Most cards are US-issued (40%). | `US`, `GB`, `DE` |

---

### Group 3: Merchant

| Column | Type | Description | Example |
|---|---|---|---|
| `MERCHANT_ID` | String (15) | 15-digit unique merchant identifier assigned by acquirer. Also called MID. | `498271047382910` |
| `MERCHANT_NAME` | String (35) | Legal name of the merchant as registered with acquirer. Max 35 characters. | `JOHNSON RETAIL GROUP INC` |
| `MERCHANT_DBA` | String (25) | **Doing Business As** name — the name as it appears on cardholder's statement. May differ from legal name. | `JOHNSON'S MART` |
| `MERCHANT_CITY` | String (20) | City of the merchant terminal location. | `SAN FRANCISCO` |
| `MERCHANT_STATE` | String (2) | US state abbreviation. Non-US merchants may have province/region codes. | `CA` |
| `MERCHANT_COUNTRY` | String (2) | ISO country code where the transaction occurred. | `US` |
| `MERCHANT_ZIP` | String (5) | ZIP/postal code of merchant location. Used in AVS (Address Verification). | `94102` |
| `MCC` | String (4) | **Merchant Category Code** — ISO 18245 four-digit code classifying the merchant type. Critical for fraud scoring and interchange rate determination. | `5411` |
| `MCC_DESCRIPTION` | String | Human-readable description of the MCC. | `Grocery Stores, Supermarkets` |
| `ACQUIRER_BIN` | String (6) | BIN of the acquiring bank (the merchant's bank). Used to identify the acquirer in dispute routing. | `414720` |
| `TERMINAL_ID` | String (8) | Unique ID of the physical POS terminal. Begins with `T`. | `T4829301` |

#### Common MCC Codes in Dataset

| MCC | Description | Fraud Risk |
|---|---|---|
| `5411` | Grocery Stores | Low |
| `5812` | Restaurants | Low |
| `7011` | Hotels, Motels | Medium |
| `3000` | Airlines | Medium |
| `6011` | Automated Cash Disburse (ATMs) | **HIGH** |
| `4829` | Money Transfer | **HIGH** |
| `7995` | Gambling | **HIGH** |
| `5999` | Miscellaneous Retail | **HIGH** |
| `5045` | Computers/Electronics Wholesale | **HIGH** |

---

### Group 4: Transaction Details

| Column | Type | Description | Example |
|---|---|---|---|
| `TXN_DATETIME` | DateTime | Full timestamp of transaction in `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS` format. Local time at point of sale. | `2024-07-15 14:32:07` |
| `TXN_DATE` | Date | Date component only. Useful for daily aggregations. | `2024-07-15` |
| `TXN_TIME` | Time | Time component only. Useful for hour-of-day analysis. | `14:32:07` |
| `POSTING_DATE` | Date | Date the transaction was posted/cleared to the cardholder's account. Typically T+1. | `2024-07-16` |
| `TXN_AMOUNT` | Decimal | Transaction amount in the local transaction currency. Always positive (even refunds use `TXN_TYPE = REFUND`). | `127.45` |
| `TXN_CURRENCY` | String (3) | ISO 4217 3-letter currency code of the transaction. `USD` for US domestic. | `USD`, `EUR`, `GBP` |
| `SETTLEMENT_AMOUNT` | Decimal | Amount after currency conversion to USD for settlement. Equal to `TXN_AMOUNT` when currency is already USD. | `142.80` |
| `SETTLEMENT_CURRENCY` | String (3) | Always `USD` in this dataset (card network settles in USD). | `USD` |
| `TXN_TYPE` | String | Type of financial transaction. Values: `PURCHASE` (88%), `REFUND` (7%), `CASH_ADVANCE` (4%), `BALANCE_INQUIRY` (1%) | `PURCHASE` |

---

### Group 5: Authorization & POS

| Column | Type | Description | Example |
|---|---|---|---|
| `RESPONSE_CODE` | String (2) | Issuer response code from the authorization message. `00` = Approved. See full code table below. | `00` |
| `RESPONSE_DESCRIPTION` | String | Human-readable description of the response code. | `Approved` |
| `IS_APPROVED` | Flag (Y/N) | Derived flag. `Y` if `RESPONSE_CODE = 00`, else `N`. 92.3% approval rate. | `Y` |
| `POS_ENTRY_MODE` | String (2) | ISO 8583 code describing how the card data was captured at the terminal. | `05` |
| `POS_ENTRY_MODE_DESC` | String | Description of the entry mode. | `Chip` |
| `POS_CONDITION_CODE` | String (2) | Describes the condition of the POS at transaction time. `00`=Normal, `01`=Unattended, `59`=Electronic Commerce | `00` |
| `TERMINAL_CAPABILITY` | String (6) | Hexadecimal code indicating terminal's cryptographic and input capabilities. | `E0F0C8` |
| `CHIP_CONDITION_CODE` | String (1) | EMV chip read status. `M`=Successfully read, `U`=Unable to read (fallback). Blank for non-chip transactions. | `M` |
| `CVV2_RESULT` | String (1) | Card Verification Value 2 check result. `M`=Match, `N`=No match, `P`=Not processed, `U`=Unsupported, `S`=Skipped | `M` |
| `AVS_RESULT` | String (1) | **Address Verification Service** result. `Y`=Full match, `A`=Address only, `Z`=ZIP only, `N`=No match, `U`=Unavailable | `Y` |
| `THREE_DS_RESULT` | String (1) | **3-D Secure** authentication result (3-D Secure). `Y`=Authenticated, `A`=Attempted, `N`=Failed. Blank if not applicable. | `Y` |
| `PIN_VERIFIED` | String (1) | Whether cardholder PIN was verified. `Y`=Verified, `N`=Not verified. Blank for card-not-present transactions. | `Y` |

#### Response Code Reference

| Code | Description | Frequency |
|---|---|---|
| `00` | Approved | ~92% |
| `05` | Do Not Honor | Most common decline |
| `51` | Insufficient Funds | Common decline |
| `14` | Invalid Card Number | Rare |
| `54` | Expired Card | Rare |
| `57` | Transaction Not Permitted | Rare |
| `61` | Exceeds Withdrawal Limit | Rare |
| `62` | Restricted Card | Rare |

#### POS Entry Mode Reference

| Code | Description | Fraud Signal |
|---|---|---|
| `05` | EMV Chip | Low risk |
| `07` | Contactless/NFC | Low risk |
| `81` | E-Commerce (card not present) | Medium-High risk |
| `91` | Contactless Mag Stripe | Medium risk |
| `10` | Credential on File / Token | Low risk |
| `71` | Magnetic Stripe Fallback | **HIGH RISK** — chip bypass |

---

### Group 6: Risk & Fraud Indicators

| Column | Type | Description | Example |
|---|---|---|---|
| `CROSS_BORDER_FLAG` | Flag (Y/N) | `Y` if `MERCHANT_COUNTRY` ≠ `ISSUER_COUNTRY`. Cross-border transactions carry higher fraud risk and different interchange rates. | `N` |
| `HIGH_RISK_MCC_FLAG` | Flag (Y/N) | `Y` if the MCC falls in a high-risk category: `7995` (Gambling), `4829` (Money Transfer), `6011` (ATM), `5999` (Misc Retail), `5045`/`5065` (Electronics Wholesale). | `N` |
| `VELOCITY_SCORE` | Integer (0–999) | Internal risk score based on transaction velocity (spend rate, geographic spread, time between transactions). Higher = more suspicious. Fraud transactions typically score 600–999. | `247` |
| `FRAUD_SCORE` | Integer (0–999) | Composite model-based fraud risk score. Derived from velocity + behavioral signals. Higher = higher fraud probability. Generally correlated with `VELOCITY_SCORE`. | `312` |
| `IS_FRAUD` | Flag (Y/N) | **Ground truth fraud label.** `Y` = confirmed fraudulent transaction. Only applies to approved transactions (declined fraud attempts are not labeled). 11.1% of total transactions. | `N` |
| `FRAUD_TYPE` | String | Category of fraud pattern. Populated only when `IS_FRAUD = Y`. See fraud type taxonomy below. | `CNP_FRAUD` |
| `FRAUD_REPORTED_DATE` | Date | Date the fraud was reported to card network (by cardholder or issuer). Typically 1–45 days after transaction date. | `2024-08-02` |

#### Fraud Type Taxonomy

| Fraud Type | Count | Description | Key Signals |
|---|---|---|---|
| `CNP_FRAUD` | 83 | **Card-Not-Present Fraud.** Stolen credentials used for online purchases. | `POS_ENTRY_MODE=81`, `CVV2_RESULT=N/U`, `AVS_RESULT=N/Z` |
| `FRIENDLY_FRAUD` | 75 | Cardholder makes legitimate purchase then disputes it. Also called "first-party fraud." | `POS_ENTRY_MODE=81`, `THREE_DS_RESULT=Y` (paradoxically authenticated) |
| `BIN_ATTACK` | 74 | Automated testing of card number combinations to identify valid cards. Small test amounts ($0.50–$2.00). | Very low `TXN_AMOUNT`, very high `VELOCITY_SCORE` (800–999) |
| `COUNTERFEIT_CARD` | 72 | Physical card cloned from stolen magnetic stripe data. | `POS_ENTRY_MODE=71` (mag stripe fallback), `CHIP_CONDITION_CODE=U` |
| `ID_THEFT` | 71 | New account or card opened using stolen identity information. | High `FRAUD_SCORE`, cross-border patterns |
| `NEVER_RECEIVED` | 69 | Legitimate card intercepted in mail before reaching cardholder. | Profile mismatch patterns |
| `LOST_STOLEN` | 66 | Physical card lost or stolen and used before cardholder reports it. | High `TXN_AMOUNT` ($500–$4,000), elevated `VELOCITY_SCORE` |
| `ACCOUNT_TAKEOVER` | 43 | Criminal gains access to legitimate account via phishing or credential stuffing. | High `VELOCITY_SCORE` (700–999) |

---

### Group 7: Chargeback / Dispute

| Column | Type | Description | Example |
|---|---|---|---|
| `HAS_CHARGEBACK` | Flag (Y/N) | `Y` if a chargeback was filed for this transaction. 6.7% of all transactions. Includes both fraud-related and non-fraud disputes. | `N` |
| `DISPUTE_ID` | String (11) | Unique dispute identifier. Format: `D` + 10 digits. Populated only when `HAS_CHARGEBACK = Y`. | `D4829301847` |
| `CB_FILED_DATE` | Date | Date the chargeback was filed by the issuer. Typically 15–90 days after transaction for fraud; 5–60 days for billing disputes. | `2024-09-14` |
| `CB_AMOUNT` | Decimal | Amount of the chargeback. Usually 80–100% of original transaction amount. | `124.50` |
| `CB_REASON_CODE` | String | card network chargeback reason code under the **Claims Resolution (VCR)** framework. See code table below. | `10.4` |
| `CB_REASON_DESCRIPTION` | String | Full description of the chargeback reason code. | `Other Fraud - Card-Absent Environment` |
| `CB_STATUS` | String | Current status of the chargeback. Values: `FILED`, `UNDER_REVIEW`, `WON_ISSUER`, `WON_MERCHANT`, `REVERSED` | `WON_ISSUER` |

#### card network Chargeback Reason Code Reference (VCR Framework)

**Category 10 — Fraud**

| Code | Description | Filed Count |
|---|---|---|
| `10.1` | EMV Liability Shift Counterfeit Fraud | 13 |
| `10.2` | EMV Liability Shift Non-Counterfeit Fraud | 21 |
| `10.3` | Other Fraud – Card-Present Environment | 14 |
| `10.4` | Other Fraud – Card-Absent Environment | 17 |
| `10.5` | Fraud Monitoring Program | 20 |

**Category 11 — Authorization**

| Code | Description | Filed Count |
|---|---|---|
| `11.1` | Card Recovery Bulletin | 14 |
| `11.2` | Declined Authorization | 13 |
| `11.3` | No Authorization | 20 |

**Category 12 — Processing Errors**

| Code | Description | Filed Count |
|---|---|---|
| `12.1` | Late Presentment | 23 |
| `12.2` | Incorrect Transaction Code | 12 |
| `12.3` | Incorrect Currency | 15 |
| `12.5` | Incorrect Transaction Amount | 20 |

**Category 13 — Consumer Disputes**

| Code | Description | Filed Count |
|---|---|---|
| `13.1` | Merchandise/Services Not Received | 24 |
| `13.2` | Cancelled Recurring Transaction | 28 |
| `13.3` | Not as Described or Defective Merchandise | 24 |
| `13.6` | Credit Not Processed | 17 |
| `13.7` | Cancelled Merchandise/Services | 28 |
| `13.9` | Non-Receipt of Cash or Load Transaction Value | 14 |

---

### Group 8: Network & Processing Flags

| Column | Type | Description | Example |
|---|---|---|---|
| `PARTIAL_APPROVAL_FLAG` | Flag (Y/N) | `Y` if the transaction was partially approved (less than the requested amount). Common in prepaid card scenarios. ~3% of transactions. | `N` |
| `RECURRING_FLAG` | Flag (Y/N) | `Y` if this is part of a recurring billing arrangement (subscriptions, utilities). ~15% of transactions. | `N` |
| `INSTALLMENT_FLAG` | Flag (Y/N) | `Y` if the transaction is an installment payment. ~5% of transactions. | `N` |
| `TOKENIZATION_FLAG` | Flag (Y/N) | `Y` if the transaction used a **network token** (network token) instead of the actual PAN. Common in Apple Pay, Google Pay, Samsung Pay. ~35% of transactions. | `Y` |
| `TOKEN_REQUESTOR_ID` | String (11) | 11-digit ID identifying the token requester (e.g., Apple, Google, Merchant). Populated only when `TOKENIZATION_FLAG = Y`. | `40010030273` |

---

## Analytical Use Cases

### Fraud Detection Modeling
- Use `IS_FRAUD` as the binary target variable
- Key predictive features: `VELOCITY_SCORE`, `FRAUD_SCORE`, `POS_ENTRY_MODE`, `CVV2_RESULT`, `AVS_RESULT`, `THREE_DS_RESULT`, `CROSS_BORDER_FLAG`, `HIGH_RISK_MCC_FLAG`, `TXN_AMOUNT`, `MCC`
- Fraud patterns vary significantly by `FRAUD_TYPE` — consider stratified analysis

### Chargeback Analytics
- Use `HAS_CHARGEBACK` and `CB_REASON_CODE` for dispute pattern analysis
- `CB_STATUS` allows win/loss rate analysis by merchant, MCC, or reason code
- Join `CB_FILED_DATE` to `TXN_DATE` to compute time-to-dispute

### Approval Rate Optimization
- Analyze `RESPONSE_CODE` distribution to identify decline categories
- Segment by `CARD_PRODUCT`, `MCC`, `ENTRY_MODE` to find optimization opportunities

### Cross-Border Risk
- `CROSS_BORDER_FLAG` combined with `ISSUER_COUNTRY` and `MERCHANT_COUNTRY` reveals geographic fraud patterns

### Tokenization Adoption
- `TOKENIZATION_FLAG` and `TOKEN_REQUESTOR_ID` can measure digital wallet adoption rates

---

## Common Queries for DataChat

- *"What is the fraud rate by MCC category?"*
- *"Show me the top 5 fraud types by transaction volume"*
- *"Which merchants have the highest chargeback rates?"*
- *"What percentage of CNP transactions have 3DS authentication?"*
- *"Compare approval rates for cross-border vs domestic transactions"*
- *"What is the average transaction amount for fraud vs non-fraud transactions?"*
- *"Show the monthly trend of chargeback filings in 2024"*
- *"Which chargeback reason codes have the highest issuer win rate?"*
- *"What is the fraud score distribution for BIN_ATTACK transactions?"*
- *"How does tokenization flag correlate with fraud rate?"*

---

## Data Quality Notes

- All PANs are synthetic and pass Luhn algorithm validation but are **not real card numbers**
- Merchant names are randomly generated; no real merchant data is represented
- Transaction timestamps are uniformly distributed across 2024
- Currency conversion rates are approximated (not real exchange rates)
- Fraud labels are deterministic based on generation rules, not probabilistic models
- A cardholder may have both fraud and non-fraud transactions in this dataset (realistic behavior)

---

*Dataset generated to mirror the card network transaction schema. For analytical and educational use only.*
demos.dashboardContext:
# card network Transaction Analytics Dashboard
### Automated Dashboard Specification — `card_transactions.xlsx`

---

## Dashboard Overview

This document defines an automated, self-refreshing analytics dashboard built on the `card_transactions.xlsx` dataset. The dashboard is organized across **5 thematic pages**, surfacing key insights across transaction health, fraud intelligence, chargeback management, merchant risk, and network trends. Each section below specifies the chart type, data source columns, key metrics, and insight narrative.

---

## Page 1: Executive Portfolio Overview

*Target audience: C-suite, Risk Officers, Portfolio Managers*

---

### KPI Scorecard (Top Banner)

| KPI | Value | Benchmark | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| Total Transactions | 5,000 | — | ✅ |
| Approval Rate | 92.3% | >90% | ✅ |
| Total Volume (USD) | $726,952 | — | ✅ |
| Fraud Rate (count) | 11.1% | <0.1% | 🔴 |
| Chargeback Rate (count) | 6.7% | <1% | 🔴 |
| Fraud Basis Points | 2,967 bps | <10 bps | 🔴 |

> **Chart Type:** KPI tiles with trend indicators  
> **Columns used:** `IS_APPROVED`, `TXN_AMOUNT`, `IS_FRAUD`, `HAS_CHARGEBACK`, `SETTLEMENT_AMOUNT`  
> **Refresh:** Recalculate on data load

---

### Monthly Transaction Volume Trend

> **Chart Type:** Dual-axis line + bar chart  
> **X-axis:** Month (Jan–Dec 2025)  
> **Primary Y-axis (bars):** Count of transactions per month  
> **Secondary Y-axis (line):** Total approved volume in USD  
> **Columns:** `TXN_DATE`, `TXN_AMOUNT`, `IS_APPROVED`  
> **Segment:** Color-split bars into Approved (blue) vs Declined (red)

**Expected Insight:** Transaction volume is uniformly distributed across 2024 (~417 transactions/month). Watch for any month-over-month spikes in decline rates.

---

### Approval Rate by Card Product

> **Chart Type:** Horizontal bar chart  
> **X-axis:** Approval rate (%)  
> **Y-axis:** `CARD_PRODUCT` (Classic, Gold, Platinum, Signature, Infinite, Business, Prepaid)  
> **Columns:** `CARD_PRODUCT`, `IS_APPROVED`  
> **Sort:** Descending by approval rate

**Expected Insight:** Infinite and Signature cards typically show higher approval rates. Prepaid cards may show lower rates due to balance constraints.

---

### Transaction Mix by Type

> **Chart Type:** Donut chart  
> **Segments:** `TXN_TYPE` (PURCHASE, REFUND, CASH_ADVANCE, BALANCE_INQUIRY)  
> **Columns:** `TXN_TYPE`  
> **Labels:** Show both count and percentage

**Expected Insight:** PURCHASE dominates at ~88%. Refunds (~7%) and Cash Advances (~4%) form a long tail with elevated fraud risk.

---

## Page 2: Fraud Intelligence

*Target audience: Fraud Analysts, Risk Operations, Data Scientists*

---

### Fraud Rate Trend — Monthly

> **Chart Type:** Line chart with threshold band  
> **X-axis:** Month  
> **Y-axis:** Fraud rate (% of transactions)  
> **Reference line:** Industry benchmark (0.10%)  
> **Columns:** `TXN_DATE`, `IS_FRAUD`  
> **Color:** Red line, yellow threshold band at 0.10%

**Expected Insight:** Fraud rate remains consistently elevated above benchmark. Identify months with spikes for root cause investigation.

---

### Fraud Volume by Type — Ranked

> **Chart Type:** Horizontal bar chart (ranked)  
> **X-axis:** Count of fraud transactions  
> **Y-axis:** `FRAUD_TYPE`  
> **Sort:** Descending by count  
> **Columns:** `FRAUD_TYPE`, `IS_FRAUD`

| Fraud Type | Count | % of Fraud |
|---|---|---|
| CNP_FRAUD | 83 | 15.0% |
| FRIENDLY_FRAUD | 75 | 13.6% |
| BIN_ATTACK | 74 | 13.4% |
| COUNTERFEIT_CARD | 72 | 13.0% |
| ID_THEFT | 71 | 12.8% |
| NEVER_RECEIVED | 69 | 12.5% |
| LOST_STOLEN | 66 | 11.9% |
| ACCOUNT_TAKEOVER | 43 | 7.8% |

**Expected Insight:** CNP and Friendly Fraud dominate. Note that Friendly Fraud is particularly challenging as 3DS authentication is present, making liability shift arguments complex.

---

### Fraud by POS Entry Mode

> **Chart Type:** Stacked bar or treemap  
> **Dimension:** `POS_ENTRY_MODE_DESC`  
> **Metrics:** Fraud count, Fraud rate within each entry mode  
> **Columns:** `POS_ENTRY_MODE_DESC`, `IS_FRAUD`  
> **Color:** Red intensity proportional to fraud rate

**Expected Insight:** E-Commerce (entry mode `81`) and Magnetic Stripe Fallback (`71`) should show significantly higher fraud rates than EMV Chip (`05`) transactions. This validates the EMV liability shift framework.

---

### Fraud Score Distribution — Fraud vs Non-Fraud

> **Chart Type:** Overlapping histogram / density plot  
> **X-axis:** `FRAUD_SCORE` (0–999 bins of width 50)  
> **Y-axis:** Transaction count  
> **Series 1:** `IS_FRAUD = Y` (red, semi-transparent)  
> **Series 2:** `IS_FRAUD = N` (blue, semi-transparent)  
> **Columns:** `FRAUD_SCORE`, `IS_FRAUD`

**Expected Insight:** Fraud transactions cluster in the 600–999 range. Non-fraud transactions peak in 100–400. The overlap zone (400–600) represents the hardest classification boundary and potential false positive risk.

---

### Cross-Border Fraud Analysis

> **Chart Type:** Matrix heatmap  
> **Rows:** `ISSUER_COUNTRY` (top 10 by volume)  
> **Columns:** `MERCHANT_COUNTRY` (top 10 by volume)  
> **Cell values:** Fraud rate (%)  
> **Color:** White → Orange → Red (low → medium → high fraud rate)  
> **Columns used:** `ISSUER_COUNTRY`, `MERCHANT_COUNTRY`, `IS_FRAUD`

**Expected Insight:** Transactions where issuer country ≠ merchant country (`CROSS_BORDER_FLAG = Y`) will show materially higher fraud rates. Specific country pairs (e.g., US cards used in certain markets) may be hotspots.

---

### Fraud by Hour of Day

> **Chart Type:** Radar chart or bar chart (24 segments)  
> **X-axis:** Hour (0–23)  
> **Y-axis:** Fraud rate  
> **Columns:** `TXN_TIME`, `IS_FRAUD`

**Expected Insight:** Fraud attempts often spike in early morning hours (2–5 AM) when cardholders are asleep and less likely to notice alerts, and in late evening for CNP fraud.

---

### CVV2 / AVS / 3DS Signal Analysis — Fraud Correlation

> **Chart Type:** Side-by-side grouped bar charts (3 panels)  
> **Each panel:** Response code values on X-axis, fraud rate on Y-axis  
> **Panel 1:** `CVV2_RESULT` (M, N, P, U, S)  
> **Panel 2:** `AVS_RESULT` (Y, A, Z, N, U, G)  
> **Panel 3:** `THREE_DS_RESULT` (Y, A, N, blank)  
> **Metric:** Fraud rate within each code  
> **Columns:** `CVV2_RESULT`, `AVS_RESULT`, `THREE_DS_RESULT`, `IS_FRAUD`

**Expected Insight:**
- CVV2 `N` (no match) should show 3–5x higher fraud rate than `M` (match)
- AVS `N` (no address match) correlates strongly with CNP fraud
- 3DS `Y` (authenticated) showing fraud = Friendly Fraud signature

---

## Page 3: Chargeback Analytics

*Target audience: Dispute Operations, Finance, Risk Management*

---

### Chargeback Volume Trend — Monthly

> **Chart Type:** Clustered bar chart  
> **X-axis:** Month (using `CB_FILED_DATE`)  
> **Y-axis:** Count of chargebacks  
> **Color split:** Fraud-related CBs (`IS_FRAUD = Y`) vs Non-fraud CBs  
> **Columns:** `CB_FILED_DATE`, `HAS_CHARGEBACK`, `IS_FRAUD`

**Expected Insight:** Chargeback filings lag transaction dates by 15–90 days. Fraudulent chargebacks filed within 30 days; billing disputes may take 45–60 days.

---

### Chargeback Reason Code Distribution

> **Chart Type:** Treemap  
> **Tile size:** Count of chargebacks per reason code  
> **Tile color:** Category grouping (10.x = red, 11.x = orange, 12.x = yellow, 13.x = green)  
> **Labels:** Reason code + count  
> **Columns:** `CB_REASON_CODE`, `CB_REASON_DESCRIPTION`

| Category | Description | Total CBs | % Share |
|---|---|---|---|
| 13.x — Consumer Disputes | Merchandise/cancellation/credit issues | 115 | 34.1% |
| 12.x — Processing Errors | Late presentment, wrong amounts | 70 | 20.8% |
| 10.x — Fraud | EMV liability shift, card-absent fraud | 85 | 25.2% |
| 11.x — Authorization | No auth, declined auth | 47 | 13.9% |

**Expected Insight:** Consumer dispute codes (Category 13) represent the largest share, particularly `13.2` (Cancelled Recurring) and `13.7` (Cancelled Merchandise) — each with 28 filings. Subscription and e-commerce merchants are highest risk.

---

### Chargeback Win/Loss Rate by Reason Code

> **Chart Type:** 100% stacked horizontal bar chart  
> **Y-axis:** `CB_REASON_CODE`  
> **X-axis:** Percentage  
> **Segments:** `WON_ISSUER`, `WON_MERCHANT`, `UNDER_REVIEW`, `FILED`, `REVERSED`  
> **Columns:** `CB_REASON_CODE`, `CB_STATUS`  
> **Color:** Green = WON_MERCHANT, Red = WON_ISSUER, Yellow = UNDER_REVIEW

**Expected Insight:** Fraud-related codes (10.x) where no 3DS or chip is present typically result in issuer wins. Merchant wins are more common for Category 13 disputes where delivery evidence can be provided.

---

### Time to Chargeback — Distribution

> **Chart Type:** Histogram  
> **X-axis:** Days from `TXN_DATE` to `CB_FILED_DATE` (0–120 days)  
> **Y-axis:** Count of chargebacks  
> **Overlay:** Separate lines for fraud vs non-fraud CBs  
> **Columns:** `TXN_DATE`, `CB_FILED_DATE`, `IS_FRAUD`

**Expected Insight:** Fraud chargebacks file within 15–30 days (cardholder discovers unauthorized charges quickly). Non-fraud/billing disputes are more spread across 30–90 days.

---

### Chargeback Rate by MCC

> **Chart Type:** Bar chart (top 15 MCCs by chargeback rate)  
> **X-axis:** `MCC_DESCRIPTION`  
> **Y-axis:** Chargeback rate (CBs / total approved txns for that MCC)  
> **Reference line:** Portfolio average CB rate  
> **Columns:** `MCC`, `MCC_DESCRIPTION`, `HAS_CHARGEBACK`

**Expected Insight:** High-risk MCCs (7995, 4829, 5999) will show CB rates well above portfolio average. Airlines (3000) and hotels (7011) also show elevated rates due to cancellation disputes.

---

### Chargeback Amount Distribution

> **Chart Type:** Box-and-whisker plot  
> **Groups:** Top 5 `CB_REASON_CODE` groups  
> **Y-axis:** `CB_AMOUNT`  
> **Show:** Median, IQR, outliers  
> **Columns:** `CB_REASON_CODE`, `CB_AMOUNT`

**Expected Insight:** Fraud chargebacks (Category 10) tend to have higher median amounts than processing error disputes (Category 12). Outliers represent high-value fraud cases.

---

## Page 4: Merchant Risk Profiling

*Target audience: Merchant Risk, Acquirer Risk, Compliance*

---

### Top 20 Merchants by Chargeback Count

> **Chart Type:** Ranked horizontal bar chart  
> **Y-axis:** `MERCHANT_NAME`  
> **X-axis:** Chargeback count  
> **Color:** Red gradient (darker = higher risk)  
> **Secondary metric:** CB rate shown as data label  
> **Columns:** `MERCHANT_NAME`, `HAS_CHARGEBACK`, `IS_APPROVED`

**Expected Insight:** Identify merchants approaching or exceeding the Chargeback Monitoring Program thresholds (1% CB ratio and 100+ CBs/month). These require remediation action.

---

### Merchant Risk Quadrant

> **Chart Type:** Scatter plot (quadrant chart)  
> **X-axis:** Fraud rate per merchant  
> **Y-axis:** Chargeback rate per merchant  
> **Bubble size:** Transaction volume (TXN_AMOUNT sum)  
> **Quadrant lines:** Portfolio average for each axis  
> **Color:** MCC risk category  
> **Columns:** `MERCHANT_NAME`, `IS_FRAUD`, `HAS_CHARGEBACK`, `TXN_AMOUNT`, `MCC`

**Expected Insight:** Top-right quadrant (high fraud + high CB) = highest-risk merchants requiring immediate investigation. Bottom-left = healthy. Outlier bubble sizes indicate high-value merchant concentration risk.

---

### Geographic Distribution of Transactions

> **Chart Type:** Choropleth map (by country) + US state-level drill-down  
> **Color intensity:** Transaction count or fraud rate  
> **Columns:** `MERCHANT_COUNTRY`, `MERCHANT_STATE`, `IS_FRAUD`

**Expected Insight:** US dominates transaction volume (~40%). International hotspots for fraud visible in cross-border transaction patterns. US drill-down reveals high-concentration states (CA, NY, TX, FL).

---

### MCC Risk Heatmap — Fraud vs Volume

> **Chart Type:** Bubble chart  
> **X-axis:** Transaction count (volume proxy)  
> **Y-axis:** Fraud rate  
> **Bubble size:** Average transaction amount  
> **Label:** MCC description  
> **Columns:** `MCC_DESCRIPTION`, `IS_FRAUD`, `TXN_AMOUNT`

**Expected Insight:** High-risk MCCs (Gambling, Money Transfer, ATM) appear in high-fraud/moderate-volume zone. Grocery and restaurants appear in high-volume/low-fraud zone. Jewelry and Airlines cluster in high-amount/medium-fraud zone.

---

## Page 5: Network & Technology Signals

*Target audience: Technology Risk, Digital Payments, Product Analytics*

---

### Tokenization Adoption Trend

> **Chart Type:** Line chart (monthly)  
> **X-axis:** Month  
> **Y-axis:** % of transactions using token (`TOKENIZATION_FLAG = Y`)  
> **Overlay:** Fraud rate for tokenized vs non-tokenized transactions  
> **Columns:** `TXN_DATE`, `TOKENIZATION_FLAG`, `IS_FRAUD`

**Expected Insight:** ~35% tokenization rate in portfolio. Tokenized transactions should show lower fraud rates, validating the security value of network tokenization (Apple Pay, Google Pay).

---

### 3DS Authentication Coverage & Efficacy

> **Chart Type:** Stacked bar chart  
> **X-axis:** Month  
> **Y-axis:** % of e-commerce transactions  
> **Segments:** Authenticated (Y), Attempted (A), Not authenticated (N), Not used (blank)  
> **Filter:** `POS_ENTRY_MODE = 81` (E-Commerce only)  
> **Columns:** `THREE_DS_RESULT`, `TXN_DATE`, `POS_ENTRY_MODE`

**Expected Insight:** 3DS coverage in e-commerce transactions. Authentication failures (N) are a key fraud vulnerability. Compare fraud rates across authentication outcomes.

---

### Authorization Response Code Waterfall

> **Chart Type:** Waterfall / funnel chart  
> **Stages:** Attempted → Approved → Non-fraud → No chargeback  
> **Columns:** `IS_APPROVED`, `IS_FRAUD`, `HAS_CHARGEBACK`

| Stage | Count | Drop-off |
|---|---|---|
| Total Attempted | 5,000 | — |
| Approved | 4,614 | -386 (7.7%) |
| Approved & Not Fraud | 4,061 | -553 (12.0%) |
| Approved, Not Fraud, No CB | 3,760 | -301 (7.4%) |
| Clean (no issues) | 3,760 | 75.2% of total |

**Expected Insight:** Only 75.2% of transaction attempts result in a completely clean record. The layered waterfall reveals where value leakage occurs.

---

### Recurring vs One-Time Transaction Risk

> **Chart Type:** Grouped bar chart  
> **Groups:** `RECURRING_FLAG` (Y/N)  
> **Metrics per group:** Approval rate, Fraud rate, Chargeback rate  
> **Columns:** `RECURRING_FLAG`, `IS_APPROVED`, `IS_FRAUD`, `HAS_CHARGEBACK`

**Expected Insight:** Recurring transactions (~15% of portfolio) have different risk profiles — lower fraud in established recurring relationships but higher `13.2` (Cancelled Recurring) chargebacks when cardholders forget to cancel subscriptions.

---

### Chip Fallback Analysis

> **Chart Type:** Comparison bar chart  
> **Groups:** Chip transactions (`POS_ENTRY_MODE = 05`) vs Mag Stripe Fallback (`71`) vs Contactless (`07`)  
> **Metrics:** Fraud rate, Average amount, Chargeback rate  
> **Columns:** `POS_ENTRY_MODE`, `IS_FRAUD`, `TXN_AMOUNT`, `HAS_CHARGEBACK`

**Expected Insight:** Mag stripe fallback should show 4–6x higher fraud rate than chip. This data quantifies the security gap and the value of terminal chip upgrade programs.

---

## Dashboard Implementation Notes

### Recommended Tools
- **Tableau / Power BI:** Import `card_transactions.xlsx`, connect directly to `Transactions` sheet
- **Python (Streamlit/Dash):** Load with `pandas`, build with `plotly.express`
- **DataChat:** Upload file, reference `instructions.md` as schema documentation; all column names are self-descriptive

### Key Calculated Fields to Pre-build

```
Fraud Rate = COUNT(IF IS_FRAUD="Y") / COUNT(ALL) * 100
Approval Rate = COUNT(IF IS_APPROVED="Y") / COUNT(ALL) * 100
Chargeback Rate = COUNT(IF HAS_CHARGEBACK="Y") / COUNT(ALL) * 100
Fraud Volume % = SUM(TXN_AMOUNT IF IS_FRAUD="Y") / SUM(TXN_AMOUNT IF IS_APPROVED="Y") * 100
Fraud BPS = Fraud Volume % * 100
Days to CB = DATEDIFF(TXN_DATE, CB_FILED_DATE)
```

### Recommended Filters (Global)
- Date range picker → `TXN_DATE`
- Card product selector → `CARD_PRODUCT`
- Transaction type → `TXN_TYPE`
- Country selector → `MERCHANT_COUNTRY`, `ISSUER_COUNTRY`
- Fraud flag → `IS_FRAUD`

### Color Convention (Consistent Across All Pages)
- 🔵 Blue: Approved / Normal transactions
- 🔴 Red: Fraud confirmed
- 🟡 Yellow/Amber: Chargeback / Disputed
- 🟠 Orange: Declined
- 🟢 Green: Resolved favorably (WON_MERCHANT)

---

*Dashboard specification v1.0 | Data: card_transactions.xlsx | Period: Jan–Dec 2025*

demos.title: NBA
demos.body: A database with information about basketball matches from the National Basketball Association. Lists Players, Teams, and matches with action counts for each player.
questions: What is the average points scored by each player in the dataset?
Which team has the highest total rebounds in a specific game?
How many assists did each player make in games where they played more than 30 minutes?
What is the shooting percentage (field goals made/field goal attempts) for each player in the dataset?
Which player has the highest plus-minus rating in a specific game?

demos.title: CraftBeer
demos.body: Craft beers labeled by styles and composition. A separate dataset lists breweries by state.
questions: What is the average ABV (Alcohol by Volume) of beers produced by each brewery?
Which brewery has the highest IBU (International Bitterness Units) beer, and what is the name of that beer?
List all beers that have an ABV greater than 8% and their corresponding breweries.
How many different beer styles are produced by breweries in a specific state (e.g., California)?
What are the top 5 breweries by the number of beers they produce, along with the count of their beers?

demos.title: Atherosclerosis
demos.body: Atherosclerosis is a longitudinal 20 years lasting primary preventive study of middle-aged men.
demos.context: The dataset contains data on individuals who participated in the Atherosclerosis study. It includes information on their demographics, medical history, and various health-related measurements over a 20-year period.
questions: What is the average weight (VAHA) of individuals with diabetes (DIABET)?
How many individuals have a history of hypertension (HYPERT) and also smoke (KOURENI)?
What are the most common causes of death (PRICUMR) recorded in the Death table?
What is the trend of cholesterol levels (CHLST) over the years for individuals with a specific condition (NEMOC1) in the Letter table?
How does the average systolic blood pressure (SYST1) compare between individuals with and without a history of heart disease (SRDCE)?

demos.title: HR: Employee Data
demos.body: Employee master data with demographics, job details, hierarchy, salary, performance, and other details.
questions: Which departments have the highest average salary and how many employees work in each?
What is the average time between hire and termination?
Which department has the highest performance, i.e. with a performance rating of 4 or above?
Which departments has the best gender balance and how many male & female employees work in each?
Who are the employees hired most recently, and what are their job titles?

demos.title: Marvel Powers
demos.body: Every Marvel character and variant along with their powers (at their weakest and strongest) from Marvel Fandom.
questions: List the powers of the top 10 characters by total power
What is the character with the highest maximum strength?
Which characters are the smartest?
How many characters have a minimum durability score greater than 50?
What is the average maximum speed of all characters listed?
Which character has the widest range between minimum and maximum energy projection?

demos.title: EHR Data
demos.body: Electronic Health Records covering patient demographics, clinical metrics, comorbidities, medications, etc. over time
questions: What is the average age of patients in the dataset?
Which comorbidity is the most common among patients?
What is the distribution of medication prescriptions by category?
How many unique patients are recorded in the dataset?
What is the trend of patient visits over time?
Which clinical metric shows the highest variance among patients?
How many patients have been prescribed more than five different medications?
What is the correlation between BMI and blood pressure levels?
Which age group has the highest incidence of diabetes?
How many patients have been admitted more than once within a year?
